component.name: PULS Kernel/GRUB Manager
component.summary: Professional bootloader and mainline kernel management tool

description.p: PULS Kernel/GRUB Manager is a specialized tool for Linux users who need fine-grained control over their system's kernel and boot process. It simplifies the process of fetching, compiling, and installing mainline kernels while ensuring system safety through snapshots and Secure Boot management.

ul.li: Mainline kernel fetching from kernel.org with GPG signature verification
Tailored compilation using localmodconfig for lean, hardware-specific kernels
Automatic DKMS rebuild for third-party modules (e.g. NVIDIA drivers)
GRUB parameter tuning and visual boot order management
Timestamped GRUB configuration backups with dry-run validation
Integrated Timeshift snapshot support before kernel changes
Machine Owner Key (MOK) management for UEFI Secure Boot
Kernel panic log analysis from pstore and kdump
Dashboard overview: running kernel, GRUB default, /boot disk usage
Kernel removal with protection against removing the running kernel
Fixed GNOME taskbar showing app ID instead of name
Fixed icon not loading when installed as a .deb package
Fixed security vulnerability in MOK password handling
Added Dashboard page with system overview cards
Added kernel removal with running-kernel protection
Added menuconfig toggle in GUI
Added Boot Once (grub-reboot) button
Added GRUB backup history viewer with per-entry restore
Added per-package dependency status display
Added streaming download progress in log dialog
Added /boot disk space check before compilation
GRUB backup rotation (keeps last 10)

description.p: Second pre-release testing phase. Fixed certificate and module loading issues.
